questions.explanation: The purchases ledger control account normally has a credit balance (money owed to creditors). A debit balance is unusual and indicates the business has overpaid a supplier or received goods on credit notes in excess of purchases.

questions.question: The capital redemption reserve arises when

options.A: A company issues new shares to the public
options.B: A company redeems (buys back) its own preference shares, maintaining the capital base by transferring from distributable reserves
options.C: A company revalues its fixed assets upward
options.D: A company converts preference shares to ordinary shares

questions.explanation: When a company redeems redeemable preference shares, it must maintain its capital base by transferring from distributable reserves (retained earnings) to the capital redemption reserve — a non-distributable reserve.

questions.question: Net Realisable Value (NRV) of stock is

options.A: The historical cost of acquiring the stock
options.B: The estimated selling price less all costs to complete and sell the stock
options.C: The replacement cost of the stock at year end
options.D: The book value of stock after depreciation

questions.explanation: NRV = Estimated selling price – Estimated costs of completion – Estimated selling costs. Stock is valued at the lower of cost and NRV under IAS 2.

questions.question: Debentures are shown on the balance sheet as

options.A: Current liabilities
options.B: Non-current (long-term) liabilities
options.C: Shareholders' equity
options.D: Current assets

questions.explanation: Debentures are long-term borrowings (loans) of a company. They are shown as non-current liabilities on the balance sheet as they are usually repayable after more than one year.

questions.question: Which of the following items is included in the trading account?

options.A: Salaries and wages
options.B: Rent and rates
options.C: Returns inwards and returns outwards
options.D: Advertising expenses

questions.explanation: The trading account includes: sales, returns inwards, cost of goods sold (opening stock, purchases, returns outwards, carriage inwards, closing stock). Salaries, rent, and advertising are P&L account items.

questions.question: Intangible fixed assets include

options.A: Land and buildings
options.B: Motor vehicles
options.C: Goodwill and patents
options.D: Fixtures and fittings

questions.explanation: Intangible fixed assets are non-physical long-term assets — examples include goodwill, patents, trademarks, copyrights, and brand names. Land, vehicles, and fixtures are tangible fixed assets.

questions.question: Long-term liabilities on a balance sheet include

options.A: Trade creditors
options.B: Bank overdraft
options.C: Accruals
options.D: Mortgage loans and debentures

questions.explanation: Long-term (non-current) liabilities are obligations payable after more than one year — examples include mortgages, debentures, and long-term bank loans. Trade creditors, overdrafts, and accruals are current liabilities.

questions.question: Partners A, B, and C share profits in the ratio 3:2:1. If net profit is ₦60,000, what is C's share?
The purpose of charging interest on drawings is to

options.A: Increase the firm's income
options.B: Discourage partners from making excessive drawings early in the year
options.C: Compensate creditors for late payment
options.D: Provide funds for replacing worn-out assets

questions.explanation: Interest on drawings compensates the partnership for partners taking money out early, as it reduces the working capital available to the business. It is charged to partners' current accounts.

questions.question: A company's authorised share capital is ₦500,000 and issued share capital is ₦300,000. The unissued share capital is
questions.explanation: Unissued share capital = Authorised share capital – Issued share capital = ₦500,000 – ₦300,000 = ₦200,000.

questions.question: Called-up share capital refers to

options.A: The full amount of shares issued to shareholders
options.B: The portion of issued share capital that shareholders have been asked to pay
options.C: Shares that have been fully paid
options.D: Shares that have not yet been allotted to shareholders

questions.explanation: Called-up capital is the portion of issued share capital that the company has requested shareholders to pay. Shares may be issued but not yet fully called up (e.g., only 75p per £1 share may be called).

questions.question: An increase in a liability is recorded as

options.A: A debit entry
options.B: A credit entry
options.C: Both a debit and a credit entry
options.D: Only in the journal

questions.explanation: According to double-entry rules, liabilities are increased by credit entries. When a liability increases, credit the liability account. Debit entries decrease liabilities.

questions.question: Revenue (income) is recorded as a

options.A: Debit entry to increase it
options.B: Credit entry to increase it
options.C: Debit entry to decrease it
options.D: Posting to the balance sheet only

questions.explanation: Revenue accounts are increased by credit entries. When a sale is made, the sales (revenue) account is credited. This increases profit, which increases capital.

questions.question: The substance over form concept means that

options.A: The legal form of a transaction always overrides its economic substance
options.B: Transactions are accounted for according to their economic reality rather than their strict legal form
options.C: Financial statements must use standardised forms approved by the government
options.D: Only formal written contracts are recorded in the books

questions.explanation: Substance over form means that financial reporting reflects the economic reality of transactions, even if this differs from the strict legal form. E.g., assets acquired under finance leases are recorded as assets despite not legally being owned.

questions.question: A machine costs ₦40,000 with a residual value of ₦4,000 and useful life of 4 years. Annual depreciation using straight-line method is
questions.explanation: Annual depreciation = (Cost – Residual value) ÷ Useful life = (₦40,000 – ₦4,000) ÷ 4 = ₦36,000 ÷ 4 = ₦9,000.

questions.question: A sole trader's capital at the start of the year is ₦80,000. During the year, net profit is ₦25,000 and drawings are ₦10,000. The closing capital is
questions.explanation: Closing capital = Opening capital + Net profit – Drawings = ₦80,000 + ₦25,000 – ₦10,000 = ₦95,000.

questions.question: If the cash book shows a balance of ₦12,000 and the bank statement shows ₦15,000, and there are no bank charges or direct credits, the most likely cause is

options.A: Outstanding deposits (uncleared lodgements) of ₦3,000
options.B: Unpresented cheques of ₦3,000
options.C: Bank charges of ₦3,000
options.D: An error in the cash book of ₦3,000

questions.explanation: Unpresented cheques of ₦3,000 have been recorded in the cash book as credits (reducing cash book balance) but have not yet been presented to the bank. So the bank statement balance (₦15,000) is higher than the cash book (₦12,000). ₦15,000 – ₦3,000 = ₦12,000.

questions.question: Subscriptions received in advance by a club are treated as

options.A: Income in the current year's income and expenditure account
options.B: A current liability on the balance sheet
options.C: A current asset on the balance sheet
options.D: A reduction of the accumulated fund

questions.explanation: Subscriptions received in advance relate to the next period. They are a current liability (deferred income) because the club has an obligation to provide membership services in the future.

questions.question: Subscriptions in arrears (unpaid by members at year end) are

options.A: Ignored in the income and expenditure account
options.B: Treated as a current asset (income receivable) on the balance sheet
options.C: Written off as a bad debt immediately
options.D: Deducted from the accumulated fund

questions.explanation: Subscriptions in arrears are income earned but not yet received. Under the accruals concept, they are included in the income and expenditure account and shown as a current asset (debtor) on the balance sheet.

questions.question: IAS 16 deals with

options.A: Inventory (stock) valuation
options.B: Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) — its recognition, measurement, and depreciation
options.C: Presentation of financial statements
options.D: Revenue recognition

questions.explanation: IAS 16 (Property, Plant and Equipment) prescribes the accounting treatment for most fixed assets — their recognition, measurement (cost or revaluation model), depreciation, and disposal.

questions.question: IAS 2 deals with

options.A: Accounting for fixed assets
options.B: Accounting for inventories (stock) — including valuation rules
options.C: Presentation of financial statements
options.D: Accounting for leases

questions.explanation: IAS 2 (Inventories) prescribes the accounting treatment for inventories, including cost measurement, the lower of cost/NRV rule, and approved cost formulas (FIFO, AVCO).

questions.question: Sales are ₦200,000 and cost of goods sold is ₦140,000. The gross profit margin is
Opening capital is ₦50,000; closing capital is ₦72,000; additional capital introduced is ₦5,000; drawings are ₦8,000. Net profit for the year is
questions.explanation: Net profit = Closing capital – Opening capital + Drawings – Capital introduced = ₦72,000 – ₦50,000 + ₦8,000 – ₦5,000 = ₦25,000.

questions.question: Goods taken by the owner for personal use are recorded as

options.A: Debit sales account; Credit drawings account
options.B: Debit drawings account; Credit purchases account
options.C: Debit expenses account; Credit drawings account
options.D: Debit capital account; Credit purchases account

questions.explanation: Goods taken by the owner are drawings at cost. Debit drawings account (reduces capital) and credit purchases account (reduces cost of purchases). This ensures profit is not understated.

questions.question: A business pays salaries of ₦60,000 per month. At year end, one month's salary is outstanding. The charge to the P&L account for salaries is
questions.explanation: Salaries charged to P&L = 12 months × ₦60,000 = ₦720,000. The outstanding ₦60,000 is an accrual (current liability). If only ₦660,000 was paid, the accrual ensures the full year's expense is charged.

questions.question: Which of the following has a credit balance in the trial balance?

options.A: Machinery account
options.B: Drawings account
options.C: Capital account
options.D: Carriage inwards account

questions.explanation: Capital account has a credit balance (owner's claim on the business). Machinery, drawings, and carriage inwards are debit balances — assets, contra-equity, and expenses respectively.

questions.question: On the admission of a new partner, any existing goodwill should be

options.A: Retained in the books at its original cost
options.B: Raised in the books (debit goodwill; credit old partners' capital accounts in the old ratio) and then written off against all partners in the new ratio
options.C: Written off directly against profits for the year
options.D: Transferred to the new partner's capital account

questions.explanation: On admission of a new partner: goodwill is raised by debiting the goodwill account and crediting old partners in their old ratio (compensating them). It is then immediately written off by debiting all partners (new ratio) and crediting goodwill.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT shown in a profit and loss account?

options.A: Depreciation
options.B: Bad debts
options.C: Drawings
options.D: Wages and salaries

questions.explanation: Drawings are not an expense of the business — they are the owner's withdrawal of capital or profits. They appear as a deduction from capital on the balance sheet, not in the profit and loss account.

questions.question: Net current assets (working capital) on a balance sheet is

options.A: Fixed assets minus current liabilities
options.B: Current assets minus current liabilities
options.C: Total assets minus total liabilities
options.D: Current assets plus current liabilities

questions.explanation: Net current assets (working capital) = Current assets – Current liabilities. This represents the liquidity cushion available to meet short-term obligations.

questions.question: The units of production (output) method of depreciation charges

options.A: The same amount each year
options.B: More in early years and less later
options.C: Depreciation based on the actual output produced by the asset in the period
options.D: Depreciation based on the remaining years of useful life

questions.explanation: The units of production method calculates depreciation per unit and charges it based on actual output: Depreciation per unit = (Cost – Residual value) ÷ Total expected output.

questions.question: Capital introduced in cash by the owner is recorded as

options.A: Debit cash account; Credit drawings account
options.B: Debit cash account; Credit capital account
options.C: Debit capital account; Credit cash account
options.D: Debit bank account; Credit profit and loss account

questions.explanation: When an owner introduces cash capital: cash increases (debit cash account) and the owner's capital increases (credit capital account).

questions.question: The balance carried down on the credit side of a cash book indicates

options.A: A favourable bank balance
options.B: A bank overdraft — the business owes money to the bank
options.C: Cash held in the business till
options.D: Interest earned on the bank account

questions.explanation: A credit balance on the bank column of the cash book means the bank account is overdrawn — the business owes money to the bank (bank overdraft). This is a liability.

questions.question: A transposition error occurs when

options.A: A transaction is completely missed from the records
options.B: The digits of a number are accidentally reversed — e.g., ₦2,400 recorded as ₦4,200
options.C: The same transaction is entered twice in the books
options.D: A debit entry is made in place of a credit entry

questions.explanation: A transposition error involves accidentally reversing the order of digits — e.g., writing 4,200 instead of 2,400. The difference between the two is always divisible by 9 (4,200 – 2,400 = 1,800; 1,800 ÷ 9 = 200).

questions.question: The cost of raw materials consumed in production is calculated as

options.A: Opening raw material stock + Purchases of raw materials – Closing raw material stock
options.B: Opening raw material stock + Purchases – Returns outwards
options.C: Total purchases of raw materials during the year
options.D: Closing raw material stock – Opening raw material stock

questions.explanation: Raw materials consumed = Opening stock of raw materials + Purchases of raw materials – Closing stock of raw materials.

questions.question: The life membership fund received by a club should be

options.A: Credited to income and expenditure account in full in the year received
options.B: Treated as a long-term liability or credited to accumulated fund over the member's lifetime
options.C: Debited to the cash account only
options.D: Returned to the member at the end of the year

questions.explanation: Life membership fees are not fully credited to income in the year received. They are credited to a life membership fund (long-term liability or part of accumulated fund) and transferred to income gradually or in total when the member dies.

questions.question: If a business has sales of ₦250,000 and closing debtors of ₦50,000, the debtors collection period (days) is

options.A: 5 days
options.B: 73 days
options.C: 50 days
options.D: 365 days

questions.explanation: Debtors collection period = (Debtors ÷ Sales) × 365 = (₦50,000 ÷ ₦250,000) × 365 = 0.2 × 365 = 73 days.

questions.question: Which accounting concept is applied when a business charges small items of stationery as an expense rather than capitalising them as fixed assets?

options.A: Consistency
options.B: Going concern
options.C: Materiality
options.D: Prudence

questions.explanation: The materiality concept allows immaterial (insignificant) items to be treated in the simplest convenient way. Small items of stationery are expensed (even though they could be fixed assets) because their cost is immaterial.
